hooks.prompt: Check whether this response completed a unit of work substantial enough to log to MEMORY.md (a shipped feature, a resolved bug, a meaningful decision, an approach that took more than two attempts). If yes, invoke the decision-log skill to append a session entry to MEMORY.md at the project root. If the response was a one-line answer, a clarifying question, an interrupted thought, or trivial work, do nothing. Be conservative — frequent low-value writes destroy the signal-to-noise of MEMORY.md.

If the user's request involves implementation work that resembles past tasks in this project (bug fix, architecture choice, library selection, performance work, refactor), silently invoke the errors-check skill in read mode before suggesting any approach. Surface a match only if confident it applies. Skip this check for one-line answers, clarifying questions, or non-implementation requests.
